summary: Matched and improved several trophy/toy routines in src/melee/ty/toy.c, with small signature/type fixes in toy.h and gm_1601.c. The decomp.dev report recorded +528 bytes of matched code and four new 100% matches in main/melee/ty/toy: un_80308354, un_803102D0, un_8030813C, and un_80309338. The PR also introduced an 82.64% implementation of un_803067BC and a tiny improvement to un_80306EEC, but left a documented broken match for un_80308250 and regressions for un_803075E8, .sdata, and .sdata2.
what_changed: Implemented previously stubbed un_803067BC in toy.c with s16 table-copy logic over un_804D6E64, un_804D6EDC, and gmMainLib_8015CC90; decomp.dev reported it improved from 0.00% to 82.64%.
Converted several OSReport plus __assert pairs in toy.c to HSD_ASSERTREPORT calls in un_80306D70, un_80307018, un_803075E8, un_8030813C, and un_80308354.

Adjusted un_80306EEC to request animation with extern f32 un_804DDCD8 instead of literal 0.0F, check position_anim instead of next, and link light objects with HSD_LObjSetNext instead of directly writing prev->next with fake dead-code assert.
Changed un_8030813C argument type from s16 to s32 in both toy.c and toy.h; the body also includes a match-shaping empty redundant condition before comparing the model table id.
Reworked un_80308354 from a partial match to a reported 100% match by using a register s32 target, explicit loop/search structure, HSD_ASSERTREPORT on failure, and labels/goto-style control flow.
Reworked un_80309338 distance/sqrt code to use extern floating constants un_804DDCD8, un_804DDD88, and un_804DDD90 instead of literals, matching the repeated __frsqrte Newton refinement sequence.
Reworked un_803102D0 to load archive symbol names via a base string pointer un_803FDD18 plus offsets instead of direct string symbols; decomp.dev reported this went from 72.38% to 100.00%.
Changed gm_801604DC and gm_80160564 local var_r3 type from s16 to s32 in src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c.

Removed toy.h extern declarations for un_80308354-specific string symbols un_803FE474, un_804D5A48, and un_804D5A50, while adding or moving extern declarations in toy.c for match-relevant constants/data.
smart_moves: Using HSD_ASSERTREPORT preserved report-and-assert behavior while matching the library's expected assert-report call shape better than separate OSReport and __assert calls.
Replacing direct prev->next assignment with HSD_LObjSetNext avoided fake dead code and aligned the source with the baselib lobj API.
Promoting function/local argument types from s16 to s32 where comparisons and table keys are treated as 32-bit values helped recover exact matches for un_8030813C and nearby callers.
Referencing extern float/double constants instead of numeric literals helped match .sdata/.sdata2 constant usage and the generated floating-point sequence in un_80309338.
For un_803102D0, using a shared string-table base pointer with offsets matched the binary's address-generation style rather than forcing individual string labels.
Keeping match-shaping artifacts visible, such as an empty redundant arg0 condition and goto labels, made the decompilation honest about compiler-control-flow requirements.
decomp_lessons: When an assert site in Melee looks like OSReport followed by __assert, check whether HSD_ASSERTREPORT is the real source-level construct; it can materially affect matching and removes fake assert scaffolding.

Header signatures matter: changing un_8030813C from s16 to s32 required updating toy.h and improved matching, but it also affected un_80308250, which decomp.dev reported as a broken match after the PR.
Direct struct-member writes can be wrong for matching if the original code used a helper; HSD_LObjSetNext produced better source for lobj linking than assigning prev->next.
Use named extern constants for common 0.0, 0.5, and 3.0 values when the binary loads them from data sections; literals may compile differently and disturb .sdata/.sdata2 matching.
String symbol access may need to follow the binary's base-plus-offset pattern rather than nicer direct labels, especially for archive load symbol tables.
A function can become 100% matched while nearby functions or data sections regress; track decomp.dev item-level results before assuming a local fix is globally neutral.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions and data retain address-based names such as un_803067BC, un_80308354, un_804D6EDC, and un_803FDD18.
Baselib/HSD API names use subsystem prefixes and CamelCase-style symbols, e.g. HSD_ASSERTREPORT, HSD_LObjSetNext, HSD_LObjAddAnimAll, HSD_LObjReqAnimAll, and HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress.
Toy/trophy-local structs use descriptive provisional names with offset-like fields, e.g. TyLightEntry, TyLightSymbol, xFC_idx, xCC_name, ToyData, and TrophyData.
Game mode functions in gm_1601.c keep gm_ address-style names such as gm_801604DC and gm_80160564 until better semantic names are known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Implemented un_803067BC with explicit s16 pointer arithmetic, reverse writes through var_r5, and an eight-element unrolled copy loop driven by count >> 3 plus a remainder loop.
Used register s32 target in un_80308354 to influence register allocation for the trophy id search.
Inserted labels and gotos around the success return and failure assert path in un_80308354 to match control-flow layout.
Added an empty redundant conditional in un_8030813C, `if ((arg0 && arg0) && arg0) {}`, as a match-shaping artifact.
Replaced float/double literals in un_80309338 with extern data symbols un_804DDCD8, un_804DDD88, and un_804DDD90 to match constant loads.
Changed un_803102D0 string references to `temp_r3 + offset` from un_803FDD18, matching the binary's string table addressing.
Changed local integer widths from s16 to s32 in gm_801604DC, gm_80160564, and un_8030813C to match generated comparisons/addressing.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code improved to 66.44%, a +0.01% / +528 byte gain.
The bot reported four new 100% matches: un_80308354 (+43 bytes), un_803102D0 (+24 bytes), un_8030813C (+12 bytes), and un_80309338 (+1 byte).
The bot reported one broken match: un_80308250 dropped from 100.00% to 98.57%.
The bot reported two improvements in unmatched items: un_803067BC from 0.00% to 82.64% and un_80306EEC from 96.39% to 96.40%.
The bot reported three regressions in unmatched/data items: un_803075E8 from 96.35% to 85.60%, .sdata2 from 68.98% to 65.53%, and .sdata from 84.54% to 68.38%.
follow_up_queries: Investigate why un_80308250 regressed from 100.00% to 98.57% after un_8030813C signature/type changes.
Revisit un_803075E8 because HSD_ASSERTREPORT/string changes caused a reported drop from 96.35% to 85.60%.
Check whether the new extern constants and string-symbol changes can recover the .sdata and .sdata2 regressions reported by decomp.dev.
Continue matching un_803067BC from 82.64% toward 100%, focusing on loop structure and s16 table copy ordering.
Audit other toy.c assert/report sites for HSD_ASSERTREPORT applicability, but verify item-level effects because this PR shows both gains and regressions.
